title: db.rs: migrations 4/7/8 ALTER TABLE not wrapped in transaction
description: db.rs WalletStore::new(): migration versions 4, 7, and 8 execute DDL (ALTER TABLE / CREATE INDEX) followed by PRAGMA user_version = N as separate calls with no explicit transaction. A crash between the DDL and the PRAGMA leaves the schema partially migrated: user_version is not advanced, so the next open re-runs the migration block and fails with 'duplicate column name' / 'index already exists'. Migration v6 correctly uses pool.begin()/commit() to wrap both the DDL and the PRAGMA. Migrations 4, 7, and 8 should do the same. SQLite DDL is only atomic when issued within an explicit BEGIN/COMMIT.
